text: In California and I guess the other states an odorant oil is added is added to the propane gas, so be prepared for that to come out. It is super messy and will draw every Blue Bottle fly within a mile or two.

author: @royhorwood2663
text: Just one little question, many, many years ago i used to work for a gas company also in kent, delivering lpg to homes, holiday sites for there swimming pools, if we had to any type of maintenance we used a copper hammer, not a steel one, as you said there is still gas inside until it is purged, and I think i am right in saying that a bottle with vapour in is even more of an explosion risk, one little spark will totally piss your day off, just saying like

author: @stephentyrrell92
text: So the fact that it's a butane cylinder and says it on the cylinder but he refers to it as propane says alot. Next if you hit the valve with something when you have already have the valve loose there is a spark potential. Please people do not listen to this guy. Not to mention the cylinder is not your property and this goes against so must HSE legislation.

author: @alankeable1937
text: At the start knocking the end around with a chisel and hammer to unscrew it, Oh so naughty, they were metal and thus a chance of a spark. Block of wood and wooden mallet - avoid the risk.

author: @michaelcorbidge7914
text: We say LPG ; that way it covers  butane or propane or the 50/50 mixture for automotive  fuels . Americans call it all propane , even if it's butane and as we know they refer to liquid fuel as gas. Rob , you should do a short video explaining it all and showing the molecules and properties and detailing LNG , LPG , CNG

author: @SteampunkGent
text: Minor problem - Calor might want their cylinder back, technically you buy the gas but the cylinder remains the property of the company that fills it.

author: @g7mks383
text: Not to be too picky that's a Butane Cylinder not Propane. Once heard of a man who was going to do this. The Police informed him the cylinders no matter where they came from  belong to and are the property of the gas company. So was damage and theft. Just worth a mention and not wanting to be a spoil sport.

author: @stevetobias4890

author: @551moley
text: As a gasman, beware the bottles are the property of the supplier  and they are currently losing a lot of bottles for one reason or another (scrap and overseas) and they aren't very happy......I say make as many log burners and BBQ as you like 👍 and if you know any older folks using bulk or bottle gas check the price they pay I regularly see people getting ripped off because they don't know how to change supplier, Haggle for best fixed price I've seen people save 50% and sometimes £100  credit/free gas. and change or threaten to change every two years.

author: @totherarf
text:
Kind of important to realize not all cylinders are the same!
This is fine for your LPG, Propane type but if you got hold  of an Acetylene or an Oxygen cylinder it is a totally different beast!

Basically Don't Touch them! ...... The Acetylene is full of Kapok and the Oxy will make a great day bad if you have something as innocuous as grease around!

author: @C-M-E
text: I was waiting to hear the water trick and was not disappointed. Oddly enough, some of these have the rubber bladder inside and some are just straight steel. The bladder ones definitely need the water soak due to their propensity for absorbing propane and choosing to 'vent' at inopportune times, like when you get your grinder at max anger and sparks are going everywhere. They do make excellent bodies for all sorts of things though, like forges and pressure chambers.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they are amazingly useful that's for sure

author: @karlmyers6518
text: Great tips, I have a large one ready to make a patio log burner or bbq

author: @ryanlebeck259
text:
Alternatively once the water is in and the gas is out you can boil the water out of the tank to reduce the weight. You can do that with metal where you can't with plastic, so I feel it is worth honorable mention.

Fantastic video mate, I'm thinking about touring our local scrap yard for parts now 😁

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's a lot of water mate - it wold take a while to boil it out - but it is a great idea for drying the inside if you want the thing whole and dry

replies.author: @ryanlebeck259
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering you could bypass the few days of draining the tank by just adding the water and then boil half out and you have the same time table to work with. Thank you for the support of my idea though.

author: @swagswap
text: What's the advantage to waiting a day before filling the tank with water?

replies.author: @karlmyers6518
replies.text: It's just another fail safe , if it's all out by doing the water method but you get an air bubble / lock then it could still be quite a bang. I've heard of people doing just water and getting a surprise. Better be safe than sorry when dealing with this stuff

author: @ChristmasEve777
text: If you're unable to open the tank for whatever reason, another option is to use a vacuum pump.  Since you can't extract 100% of the contents that way, you can then fill it with an inert gas afterwards (like nitrogen), release the pressure, fill it again, release it, just to carry out some of those remaining molecules.  Or, instead of nitrogen, use the gas you'll be storing in it to flush it a few times.

author: @markhighway2540
text: Fill it with water

author: @Nuts-Bolts
text: “I can already smell the propane” he says holding a blue cylinder of butane, which is an odour less gas. Lets try again: ‘I can already smell the marcaptans indicating there maybe some butane left in the vessel.’    🧐

replies.author: @mikebond6328
replies.text: Wonder how you would know the difference if someone had filled the butane tank with propane.

author: @twestgard2
text: A plastic hose can siphon the water out of the tank. It’s not quick but it’s easier than trying to move a big heavy water bottle.

replies.author: @ChristmasEve777
replies.text: And with a long hose, you could siphon the water far away from the tank if you can't get that area wet.
